entries.title: Worktree-First Development Workflow
entries.verdictRationale: 65-70% implemented across wt-* skills and agent leaders. Remaining gaps are minor wiring issues (draft PR creation, gh pr ready call). Plan references obsolete filesystem architecture (CHECKPOINT.yaml as files, FEATURE_DIR directories, hyphenated status names).

validity.notes: Core workflow already implemented in pm-story-generation-leader Phase 5.5, git-ops agent, qa-verify-completion-leader. Architecture shifted from .claude/commands/ to .claude/agents/ leader/worker pattern.

implemented: wt-new creates worktrees at story creation (pm-story-generation-leader Phase 5.5)
wt-commit-and-pr repurposed for incremental pushes to existing draft PRs
wt-merge-pr squash-merges and cleans up worktree on QA PASS
git-ops agent has create-draft-pr and promote-pr actions
CHECKPOINT.yaml stores pr_number and pr_url via KB artifacts
Worktree DB tracking via MCP tools (worktree_register, worktree_get_by_story)
notImplemented: wt-new doesn't auto-create draft PR (git-ops has the action but it's not wired)
pm-story Phase 5.5 creates worktree but not draft PR
No code-review completion step calls gh pr ready (promote-pr exists but nothing invokes it)
Leader-only commit policy documented but not enforced in agent guidelines

staleReferences: .claude/commands/*.md referenced as primary — agents (.claude/agents/) are now the orchestration layer
CHECKPOINT.yaml assumed as filesystem file — now KB artifacts via kb_read_artifact/kb_write_artifact
FEATURE_DIR pattern throughout — stories are KB-only, no filesystem directories
/story-move {FEATURE_DIR} {STORY_ID} — still works but kb_update_story_status() is authoritative
architectureChanges: Update to KB artifact model (kb_read_artifact/kb_write_artifact) instead of filesystem CHECKPOINT.yaml
Use correct KB state names: ready_for_review (not needs-code-review), in_qa (not uat)
Reference agent leader/worker pattern instead of monolithic command files
newPatterns: LangGraph orchestrator integration (dev-implement.ts graph) not mentioned in plan
Worktree MCP tools (worktree_register, worktree_get_by_story, worktree_mark_complete) not referenced
KB-first state management via kb_update_story_status() not referenced

challengesSustained.challenge: Draft PR creation — the core feature — is NOT wired. That's the headline deliverable, not a minor gap.
challengesSustained.resolution: Sustained — but the infrastructure exists (git-ops has create-draft-pr action). It's a wiring task, not a design task. Verdict still holds as ARCHIVE since the plan itself is stale.

challengesOverruled.challenge: dev-implement-story.md Step 1.3 still reads CHECKPOINT.yaml at line 366
challengesOverruled.resolution: Overruled — it reads via kb_read_artifact now, not filesystem. The field name persists but the mechanism changed.

challengesOverruled.challenge: .claude/commands/ files still exist and are actively used
challengesOverruled.resolution: Overruled — commands exist as entry points but delegate to agents. The plan's proposed changes target the command files which are now thin wrappers.

devilsAdvocate.confidenceAdjustment: Verdict stands at ARCHIVE. Devil's advocate confirmed the core finding: plan is mostly implemented but the flagship feature (auto-draft-PR) needs wiring. However, the plan document itself is too stale to be useful as implementation guidance — better to create fresh stories for remaining gaps.

decisions.question: Should remaining gaps (draft PR wiring, gh pr ready) be tracked as new stories?
decisions.recommendation: Create 2-3 focused stories under an existing plan — these are small wiring tasks, not a plan-level effort
decisions.choice: Archive plan, defer remaining gaps — not blocking current work

recommendedActions: Archive this plan and its duplicate (sequential-dancing-lobster)
Create focused stories for: wire draft PR into wt-new, add gh pr ready to code-review completion
No plan rewrite needed — remaining work is tactical, not architectural

actionsTaken.action: Updated duplicate plan status to archived

entries.title: Worktree-First Development Workflow
entries.verdictRationale: Duplicate of worktree-first-draft-pr-lifecycle with identical title and overlapping content. No unique content — same plan ingested under auto-generated slug.

validity.notes: Pure duplicate. Summary even references the original slug: 'Absorbs: worktree-first-draft-pr-lifecycle'.

challengesOverruled.challenge: The summary says 'Absorbs' the original — maybe this is the consolidated/canonical version?
challengesOverruled.resolution: Overruled — both plans have identical scope. The 'Absorbs' note indicates this was meant to replace the original but neither was cleaned up. Both should be archived.

devilsAdvocate.confidenceAdjustment: Verdict confirmed. This is unambiguously a duplicate.

entries.title: Update Turbo Dev Command
entries.verdictRationale: 100% implemented. Every change described in the plan is already present in turbo.json and package.json. The plan proposed 3 changes (turbo.json task config, package.json script simplification, Docker check) — all 3 are verbatim in the codebase. Duplicate plan (agile-exploring-karp) also exists and should be archived.

validity.notes: The problem (messy concurrently-based dev script) has been fully solved. turbo.json has the //#infra:up task and dev dependency chain. package.json has the exact scripts proposed. Docker check is present in infra:up.

implemented: //#infra:up task in turbo.json with cache: false — evidence: turbo.json:9-11
dev task with dependsOn //#infra:up, cache: false, persistent: true — evidence: turbo.json:25-29
dev script uses turbo dev --filter=@repo/lego-api --filter=@repo/main-app — evidence: package.json:27
infra:up with Docker availability check — evidence: package.json:30
infra:reset script with -v flag — evidence: package.json:32
concurrently removed from dev script (no longer used for orchestration) — evidence: package.json:27
infra/compose.lego-app.yaml exists as referenced — evidence: infra/compose.lego-app.yaml

challengesOverruled.challenge: The plan says to remove concurrently usage — it's still listed as a devDependency in package.json line 80
challengesOverruled.resolution: Overruled — the plan's scope was to remove concurrently from the dev script orchestration, not to uninstall the package. The dev script no longer uses concurrently. The package may be used by other scripts or is a leftover that knip would catch. Not a plan gap.

challengesOverruled.claim: Plan is 100% complete — nothing left to do
challengesOverruled.challenge: Workspace packages may still have dev scripts using old patterns (concurrently, manual docker). Were all workspace package.json files checked?
challengesOverruled.resolution: Overruled — the plan explicitly scopes to 2 files (turbo.json and root package.json). Workspace packages have their own dev scripts that are orchestrated by Turbo, which is exactly what the plan intended. No workspace packages reference concurrently in their dev scripts.

devilsAdvocate.confidenceAdjustment: Verdict confirmed at ARCHIVE. All challenges overruled with evidence. This plan is unambiguously fully implemented.

decisions.question: Should the duplicate plan (agile-exploring-karp) also be archived?
decisions.recommendation: Archive both — identical content, both fully implemented

decisions.question: Should the concurrently devDependency be removed as cleanup?
options: Leave it — knip will flag if unused
decisions.recommendation: Leave it — out of scope for this plan, knip will catch it if truly unused

recommendedActions: Archive duplicate plan (agile-exploring-karp) — same content, auto-generated slug
Optionally run knip to check if concurrently can be removed from devDependencies
